steps: Launch the app via scripts/launch-toaster-monitored.ps1.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Confirm a preview pane is visible above the caption controls with a static background frame and a caption pill on top.
Confirm no camera/microphone permission prompt is raised by the OS or the webview.
Open features/caption-settings-preview/PRD.md and confirm the 'Out of scope' section lists camera-based preview and names the follow-up slug 'caption-settings-camera-preview'.
Open features/caption-settings-preview/BLUEPRINT.md and confirm the same follow-up slug is named under Architecture decisions R-001.
Launch the app via scripts/launch-toaster-monitored.ps1.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Confirm the preview pane is the first element in the caption section, immediately above the first caption control (font size).
Confirm no other caption control is above the preview pane within the caption section.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Scroll the caption section until the last caption control (e.g. font family) is fully visible.
Confirm the preview pane remains visible at the top of the section (sticky) and does not overlap the section heading.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Resize the app window from wide to narrow (e.g. 1600px down to 720px).
Confirm the preview pane keeps a 16:9 aspect ratio (no horizontal letterboxing inside the pane) and shrinks/grows in lockstep with the panel width.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Confirm the caption pill in the preview pane shows the literal text 'looking crispy' on first open.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Locate the sample-text dropdown above the preview and confirm it offers exactly three options: short, two-line, long.
Select 'short' and confirm the pill renders 'looking crispy' on a single visible line.
Select 'two-line' and confirm the pill renders text wrapping to exactly two visible lines.
Select 'long' and confirm the pill renders text wrapping to three or more visible lines without overflowing the pane.
Temporarily rename eval/fixtures/toaster_example.mp4 (or the bundled first-frame PNG asset, whichever the implementation imports).
Restart the app via scripts/launch-toaster-monitored.ps1 and open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Confirm the preview pane renders a flat #1a1a1a background and the caption pill still appears.
Open the webview devtools console and confirm no errors were logged from the preview pane.
Restore the renamed asset.
Open src/components/settings/CaptionSettings.tsx and confirm it imports the caption pill renderer (CaptionPill or CaptionOverlay) from src/components/player/CaptionOverlay.tsx.
Confirm the only new file under src/components/ for this feature is the preview-pane wrapper inside CaptionSettings.tsx (or a single sibling file).
Run: rg --files src | rg -i 'Caption.*Render|CaptionPill' to enumerate caption-renderer files.

Confirm the only matches are inside src/components/player/CaptionOverlay.tsx (the canonical renderer) and that no new file matching src/**/Caption*Render*.{ts,tsx} or src/components/settings/CaptionPill*.{ts,tsx} has been added.
Open src/components/settings/CaptionSettings.tsx and the new preview-pane code path; confirm caption settings are read via the existing useSettings hook (line 3 of CaptionSettings.tsx).
Confirm no new Zustand store, no new React Context, and no derived snapshot of caption settings has been introduced for the preview.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Drag the font-size slider slowly across its full range while watching the preview.
Confirm the pill resizes in lockstep with the cursor (no perceptible lag) and that releasing the slider does not cause a delayed 'settle' frame.
Run: rg -n 'debounce|throttle|setTimeout|requestIdleCallback' src/components/settings/CaptionSettings.tsx src/components/player/CaptionOverlay.tsx
Confirm there are zero matches in the code paths that connect useSettings to the rendered CaptionPill props.
Open Settings -> Advanced -> Captions.
Drag the background-opacity slider; confirm the pill background opacity tracks the cursor with no lag.
Drag the radius slider; confirm the pill corner radius tracks the cursor with no lag.
Change the font-family dropdown; confirm the pill font changes within the same frame as the dropdown UI updates.
